notes: SUN 12-card gravity deck. The garden in June working on Frank: the small pressures of a long afternoon — the heat, the gate that looks close, the small itch to leave.

cards.title: THE SHADOW MOVED A FINGER
cards.flavor: Of the oak. Across the brick. One finger's width. You can measure it without looking up.

effects.text: A finger. Two hours and fifty-eight minutes to go.

cards.title: THE BENCH IS HOT
cards.flavor: Under your right thigh in particular. The wood has been in the sun since noon. The slats keep what the sun gives.

then.text: You shift your weight. The bench is the bench.

cards.title: PEPPER FINDS YOU
cards.flavor: The Bouchon's spaniel. She works the benches around the garden in a circle every afternoon. She lands at yours about two-thirty.

cards.title: THE KINNELL IN YOUR LAP
cards.flavor: You opened to 'St. Francis and the Sow' for the third Wednesday running. You read the line you have been reading every Wednesday. The line does what the line does.

cards.title: A GRANDFATHER AT THE SOUTH BENCH
cards.flavor: Newspaper folded in his hand. He has come from the north entrance; he has been heading to the central oak's bench because that is the bench. He's looking at yours.

cards.title: THE GROUNDSKEEPER'S TRUCK
cards.flavor: Mr Bouchon's white F-150 pulls up at the gate. He's here Wednesdays for the watering. He'll be at the NE flowerbed by three-fifteen.

cards.title: THE GATE LOOKS CLOSE
cards.flavor: Forty paces. Out through the pergola. Out to the parking lot. Your car. The drive home. The short way out.

cards.title: THE WATER IN YOUR BOTTLE
cards.flavor: You brought a half-litre. It is, by 2:30, the temperature of the bench.

cards.title: YOU REMEMBER THE NOVEMBER WALKS
cards.flavor: After Susan went. The first month here was the November walks. The bench then was the south bench because the oak was bare and you wanted the sun on you.

cards.title: THE SUNDIAL IS AT THREE
cards.flavor: Three o'clock by the gnomon's shadow. The bronze plate has the right brass-line.

then.text: You read it. Three. Two hours and eighteen minutes to go.

cards.title: THE OAK CREAKS ONCE
cards.flavor: Above you. Old wood in a small load shift. The tree has been here for two hundred years and creaks like a tree that has been.

effects.text: Two hundred years. The creak is the right creak.

cards.title: 5:18 PM
cards.flavor: Three hours. The shadow has moved its three hours. The work, the work of sitting, has been done.

effects.text: [b]Three hours.[/b] You stand. The bench, after you, will keep what you left in it.
